title: STANDARD FOR A BLEND OF EVAPORATED SKIMMED MILK AND VEGETABLE FAT CXS 250-2006

sections.section: STANDARD FOR A BLEND OF EVAPORATED SKIMMED MILK AND VEGETABLE FAT CXS 250-2006
sections.summary: Document title and history: Codex Standard for a Blend of Evaporated Skimmed Milk and Vegetable Fat CXS 250-2006, adopted 2006 and amended in 2010, 2018, 2021 and 2022.

Scope: Applies to blends of evaporated skimmed milk and vegetable fat (also called unsweetened condensed skimmed milk and vegetable fat) intended for direct consumption or further processing, as described in Section 2.

Description: The product is prepared by recombining milk constituents with potable water or by partially removing water and adding edible vegetable oil/fat (or mixtures) to meet the compositional requirements in Section 3.

Essential composition and quality factors: Raw materials include skimmed milk, skimmed milk powders, other non‑fat milk solids and edible vegetable fats/oils with allowed milk retentate, milk permeate and lactose for protein adjustment; permitted ingredients include potable water and sodium/potassium chloride; fortification with vitamins/minerals must follow CXG 9‑1987 and national legislation; compositional minimums are specified for standard blend (min fat 7.5% m/m, MSNF 17.5% m/m, milk protein in MSNF 34% m/m) and reduced‑fat blend (fat >1% and <7.5% m/m, MSNF min 19% m/m, milk protein in MSNF 34% m/m).

Raw materials: Permitted raw materials are skimmed milk, skimmed milk powders and other non‑fat milk solids, and edible vegetable fats/oils, with milk retentate (ultrafiltration concentrate), milk permeate (protein/fat‑removed permeate) and lactose allowed for protein adjustment.
Permitted ingredients: Only potable water and sodium chloride and/or potassium chloride (as a salt substitute) are permitted ingredients.

Permitted nutrients: Addition of vitamins A, D and other nutrients must follow the General Principles for the Addition of Essential Nutrients to Food (CXG 9‑1987) and national legislation, which should set maximum/minimum levels or prohibit specific nutrients as appropriate.
Composition (general): The Standard sets distinct compositional requirements for the standard blend and for a reduced‑fat blend, detailed in the following subsections.

sections.section: 3.4 Blend of evaporated skimmed milk and vegetable fat
sections.summary: Blend of evaporated skimmed milk and vegetable fat: For the standard blend the minimum compositional requirements are: total fat 7.5% m/m, milk solids‑not‑fat (MSNF) 17.5% m/m, and milk protein in MSNF 34% m/m.

sections.section: 3.4 Reduced fat blend of evaporated skimmed milk and vegetable fat
sections.summary: Reduced fat blend: For reduced‑fat products total fat must be more than 1% and less than 7.5% m/m, with minimum MSNF 19% m/m and milk protein in MSNF 34% m/m.

Permits only additive classes marked as 'technologically justified' in the table for the specified product categories; acidity regulators may be used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives for beverage whiteners (category 01.3.2), and only certain acidity regulators, emulsifiers, stabilizers and thickeners from Table 3 are acceptable.

Requires total fat content to be declared to the final consumer either as a percentage by mass or volume or in grams per serving with number of servings stated, and mandates a label statement indicating presence of edible vegetable fat/oil and, where required by the country of retail sale, the common name of the vegetable source.

Requires declaration of milk protein content to the final consumer either as a percentage by mass or volume or in grams per serving with the number of servings stated, in a manner acceptable in the country of sale.

Allows omission from the ingredient list of milk products used solely for protein adjustment, notwithstanding Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Requires an advisory label statement indicating the product should not be used as a substitute for infant formula (e.g., 'NOT SUITABLE FOR INFANTS').
States that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Mandates that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this Standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the Standard's provisions.
